summary: generate_final_answer() in agents/eval/pipeline.py is genuinely the sole final-answer call path for all three arms, is a verbatim reuse (not reimplementation) of query.synthesizer.synthesize_answer, and the identical-call-path guarantee was independently mutation-tested and confirmed enforced by the test suite. One accuracy issue found in the commit message's claimed test counts for agents/query+agents/llm (does not affect eval/ correctness or this diff's own tests).

requirements_conformance.evidence: Acceptance criteria: all three arms produce final answer via exact same agents/llm/ call path, only retrieval varies. Confirmed: run_hivemind_arm, run_vector_rag_arm, run_graphrag_lite_arm each perform only their own retrieval (or accept pre-retrieved ids) then call the single generate_final_answer(...) function, which in turn calls query.synthesizer.synthesize_answer with identical positional/keyword argument order and identical model/temperature/max_tokens/timeout values across all three wrappers. query_type ('eval_benchmark') and entities (()) are literal module-level constants (_EVAL_QUERY_TYPE, _EVAL_ENTITIES) defined inside generate_final_answer's module, not parameters -- no caller can override them per-arm. Test spec file agents/eval/test_shared_final_llm.py exists with 5 tests as claimed.

architecture_conformance.evidence: docs/LLD/eval.md already declared the identical-final-answer-LLM design; the diff's one-paragraph addition documents that this is now enforced in code and points to query-agent.md#synthesizerpy and the test file -- consistent with, not contradicting, canonical docs (git diff on docs/LLD/eval.md is a clean 7-line addition, no rewrite). query.synthesizer.py itself has an EMPTY diff between parent and this commit (confirmed via git diff <parent> <commit> -- agents/query/synthesizer.py), and generate_final_answer's call site matches synthesize_answer's real signature (refined_intent, query_type, entities, selected_markdown, llm_client, *, model, temperature, max_tokens, timeout) argument-for-argument -- this is genuine reuse, not a parallel/duplicated implementation.

regression_risk.evidence: git diff <parent>..<commit> for agents/query/synthesizer.py, agents/pyproject.toml, agents/eval/baselines/vector_rag.py, vector_rag_rerank.py, graphrag_lite.py, and agents/llm/* are all EMPTY -- confirmed no existing file besides docs/LLD/eval.md (1 paragraph, additive) was modified. Full agents/eval/ suite independently re-run: 88 passed (matches claim). Only new files are agents/eval/pipeline.py and agents/eval/test_shared_final_llm.py plus CDR bookkeeping artifacts.

edge_cases.evidence: _build_selected_markdown silently skips retrieved_doc_ids absent from corpus (tested by test_build_selected_markdown_produces_expected_file_headers, including a 'doc-missing' id and verifying ranked order is preserved for present ids). Reasonable disclosed behavior for a fixture-corpus benchmark harness.

security.evidence: grep of pipeline.py and test_shared_final_llm.py for openrouter/gemini/api_key/.env found zero references outside the module's own disclosure-comment text (which explicitly states these are NOT used). pipeline.py constructs no provider client itself; llm_client is always caller-injected. Test file's _SpyLLMClient subclasses the real LLMClient interface directly (no live network I/O), consistent with Ollama-only, fixture-only design mirroring 5.2.1-5.2.3 precedent.

performance.evidence: No new dependency (agents/pyproject.toml diff confirmed empty). No additional LLM calls introduced beyond what each arm's retrieval step already required; generate_final_answer performs exactly one synthesize_answer call per invocation. Not performance-sensitive code (benchmark harness, not production hot path).

maintainability.evidence: Single shared function eliminates 3x duplication risk. Extensive module/function docstrings disclose design rationale (reuse-not-reinvention, literal constants, Ollama-only, HiveMind scope boundary) which aids future maintainers. ruff check agents/eval/pipeline.py agents/eval/test_shared_final_llm.py: All checks passed.

test_coverage.evidence: MUTATION TEST PERFORMED: I directly edited agents/eval/pipeline.py to insert a hidden per-caller branch inside generate_final_answer (temperature forced to 0.37 whenever retrieved_doc_ids has length 1 and does not contain the substring 'graph' -- simulating a hypothetical hidden per-arm divergence that would NOT be caught by the 'structural' call-count test alone). Re-ran agents/eval/test_shared_final_llm.py: 2 of 5 tests failed as expected (test_synthesize_answer_invoked_identically_across_arms and test_final_answer_call_signature_identical_across_arms both caught temperature==0.37 != 0.0). This confirms the 'observational' tests are genuinely strong (compare captured call kwargs across all three arms), not merely structural call-count checks, and would catch a hidden per-arm divergence inside generate_final_answer itself. Mutation was reverted; git diff on agents/eval/pipeline.py confirmed empty afterward. Independently re-ran the full new test file (5/5 passed) and the full agents/eval/ suite (88 passed) against the actual committed code.

confidence.evidence: One discrepancy found in the commit message's claimed test results for agents/query + agents/llm ('169 passed / 2 deselected'). Independently running `python -m pytest query/ llm/ -q` on the actual committed code produces a full collection ERROR (agents/query/test_server.py fails to import due to a protobuf gencode/runtime version mismatch: gencode 6.33.5 vs runtime 5.29.6), which aborts the whole run rather than yielding 169 passed. Running with --ignore=query/test_server.py instead yields 171 passed, 5 failed (all 5 failures in agents/query/test_wiring.py, same protobuf VersionError). Neither the exact pass count (169) nor the exact deselected count (2) match what I observed (171 passed / 5 failed, plus 1 file that cannot even be collected) in this environment. HOWEVER: (a) this same root cause (protobuf gencode 6.33.5 vs runtime 5.29.6) is independently documented as a pre-existing, environment-level issue in prior verification run .cdr/runs/2026-07-11/10010-verification/verification.json (5.2.1's own verification), which found the identical failure class in query/test_wiring.py, query/test_server.py, ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py, ingestion/test_shortlist.py and explicitly did not hold it against that verdict; (b) none of the affected files (query/test_server.py, query/test_wiring.py, query/server.py, query/wiring.py) are touched by this diff at all -- this commit only adds agents/eval/pipeline.py, agents/eval/test_shared_final_llm.py, and a docs paragraph; (c) since this run's HEAD IS the commit under test and the same failures reproduce, this confirms the issue predates and is unrelated to 5.2.4's changes, not a regression it introduced. This is a documentation-accuracy finding about the commit message's specific claimed numbers, not a code defect or a regression risk from this diff -- does not block the eval/ suite's own correctness (88/88 confirmed exactly as claimed).

findings.description: Commit message claims 'agents/query + agents/llm suites 169 passed / 2 deselected' but independent re-run on the exact same commit shows either a full collection ERROR (if query/test_server.py is included) or 171 passed / 5 failed (if it is ignored) -- neither matches the claimed numbers exactly. Root cause (protobuf gencode 6.33.5 vs runtime 5.29.6 local environment mismatch) is confirmed pre-existing and unrelated to this diff via prior verification precedent (10010-verification, for 5.2.1) which documented the identical failure class in the same files before this commit existed. Recommend correcting the commit message's precision in future subtasks (state 'known environment-dependent failures, count varies by local protobuf pin' rather than an exact number that does not reproduce) but this does not block PASS for 5.2.4 itself since agents/eval/ (the only suite this diff touches) is exactly 88/88 as claimed.

run_hivemind_arm accepts a pre-retrieved retrieved_doc_ids list rather than invoking the real production agents/query/pipeline.py's run_query_pipeline() (gRPC-backed) live. This is explicitly disclosed in pipeline.py's module docstring under 'HiveMind arm -- scope boundary, disclosed', correctly scoped to this subtask's stated impacted modules (agents/eval/pipeline.py + its test only), and does not silently misrepresent the HiveMind arm -- it is a known, named limitation with a stated path for a future subtask to wire it to real retrieval. Acceptable disclosed limitation, not a defect.

mutation_test.description: Injected a hidden per-caller temperature divergence inside generate_final_answer keyed on retrieved_doc_ids shape, to simulate a bug that only a genuinely comparative (not merely structural call-count) test could catch.
mutation_test.result: 2 of 5 tests failed as expected (test_synthesize_answer_invoked_identically_across_arms, test_final_answer_call_signature_identical_across_arms), confirming the test suite is NOT merely structural and would catch a real per-arm divergence.

independent_test_reruns.query_llm_ignoring_test_server: 171 passed, 5 failed (pre-existing protobuf gencode/runtime mismatch, confirmed unrelated to this diff)

issue_closable_note: This is the final subtask (5.2.4) of issue #27; 5.2.1-5.2.3 are already merged per git log (573478a, 3b8e8d3, 2697d07) and this subtask verifies PASS_WITH_COMMENTS with no blocking findings. Note: the GitHub issue body's subtask checkboxes are all still shown unchecked ([ ]) as of this review -- that is an issue-body bookkeeping matter, not a code defect, and should be updated when closing #27.
